---
title: 'Protein expression (kidney): IHC'
subtitle: 'Portfolio targets'
date: '`r format(Sys.time(), "%d %B, %Y")`'
author: "Target Sciences <max.salm@bicycletx.com>"
format: 
  html:
    theme: 
        light: sandstone
        dark: darkly
    toc-title: Contents
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 2
    toc-location: right
    code-fold: true
    number-sections: true
    number-depth: 2
    highlight-style: pygments
    smooth-scroll: true
    link-external-icon: true
    link-external-newwindow: true    
    citations-hover: true
    footnotes-hover: true
    embed-resources: true    
bibliography: references.bib
csl: nature.csl
citeproc: true
lightbox: auto
lang: en-GB
pagetitle: 'ihc_healthy'
description-meta: 'Exploratory Data Analysis'
author-meta: 'm.salm'
date-meta: '`r format(Sys.time(), "%d %B, %Y")`'
---

![Department: Therapeutics (Target Sciences)](./IMG/Bicycle_Workmark_RGB_Reg.jpg){width=50% fig-align="left"}



```{r child = '01b-frontMatter.Rmd'}
```

# Aim

To identify targets at risk of on-target off-tumour toxicity in kidney by clustering IHC data across targets

# Results

::: {.panel-tabset}

To quantify colour profiles in immunohistochemistry data from healthy human kidney tissue samples in the Human Protein Atlas, we retrieved 9422 immunohistochemistry images for 3323 protein targets annotated to the following sub-cellular locations: plasma membrane, junction, or Focal adhesion sites. Kidney-specific proteins ("NPHS1", "SLC22A13", "SLC12A1", "AQP2") were included as positive [controls](https://www.proteinatlas.org/humanproteome/tissue/kidney). 


## Summarising Colour

After removing the white background (and applying a simple contrast enhancement), we extracted image pixels from each image to produce a multidimensional fixed-bin colour histogram for each image [@Weller2019]. To compare colour palettes between images, the Earth Mover's distance was calculated between histograms.

The distance matrices were clustered using agglomerative hierarchical clustering. 


```{r, fig.height=13, fig.width=32}
#| label: fig-ihc_bp
#| fig.showtext: TRUE
#| fig-cap-location: bottom
#| fig-cap: "A stacked barplot of staining intensity, ordered by proportion of pixels with a high staining intensity. Currently unfiltered so very large."

targets::tar_load(ihc_bp)
plot(ihc_bp)
```

```{r, fig.height=32/2, fig.width=32/2}
#| label: fig-img_dist
#| fig.showtext: TRUE
#| fig-cap-location: bottom
#| fig-cap: "Heatmap of color distance matrices, clustered by similarity. Colour histograms are extracted from each image (after background removal), and the average histogram estimated for antibody replicates, before estimating the earth mover’s distance between protein targets. Dark blue is more similar, magenta is more dissimilar."

# targets::tar_load(img_dist)
# colordistance::heatmapColorDistance(img_dist, main="Target similarity by IHC in kidney tissue")

targets::tar_load(ihc_hm)
ComplexHeatmap::draw(
	ihc_hm, 
	heatmap_legend_side = "top", 
	annotation_legend_side = "top"
)


```


## Summarising Visual Content

A pre-trained Inception-V3 model was used to extract feature embeddings across all images, and Euclidean distance between embedding vectors calculated. The distance matrices were clustered using agglomerative hierarchical clustering. 

```{r, fig.height=13, fig.width=32}
#| label: fig-embed_dend
#| fig.showtext: TRUE
#| fig-cap-location: bottom
#| fig-cap: "The first dimension of the PCA captures staining intensity related information. In the second plot, we cluster the Euclidian distance between antibodies. N.B. Whilst the clustering by target is encouraging, this may well be the result of a systematic batch effect that needs to be investigated further."

targets::tar_load(pca_plt)
plot(pca_plt)

targets::tar_load(embed_dend)
plot(embed_dend)

```

## Summary table


```{r}
targets::tar_load(ihc_tb)
library(gt)
library(gtExtras)
ihc_tb |>
  dplyr::mutate(prop = round(stain_proportion * 100)) |>
  gt() |>
  gtExtras::gt_theme_guardian() |>
	gt::tab_source_note("Source: The Human Protein Atlas (v24.0); Wahl et al (2021)") |>
  gtExtras::gt_plt_bar_pct(
    column = prop,
    scaled = TRUE,
    fill = "#0097E5") |>
  gt::opt_interactive()
```


:::

# Methods

## Datahub: Human Protein Atlas

The development version of `HPAanalyze` [@Tran2019] was used to retrieve immunohistochemistry (IHC) images, and granular metadata [@Uhlen2010; @Uhlen2015; @Uhlen2019a; @Uhlen2019; @Uhlen2017; @Karlsson2021; @Sjoestedt2020; @Thul2017; @Yuan2025]. Searches were restricted to healthy kidney tissue (snomed M-00100).

The `colordistance` package [@Weller2019] was used to extract color histograms (3 bins per HSV channel) from IHC data. Color histograms were averaged for antibody replicates. The distance between color profiles was represented as the Earth Mover Distance, so that the final distance reflects the similarity of the clusters in both size and color.




```{r child = '06-footer.Rmd'}
```
